Level 1 Groups 14 6 Test Cases 14 7 Questions and Answers 14 Enhancement Revision History Changed By Date Version Notes: Connor Mairena 04 / 18 /2020 0.0 Original document. Kelly Faria 05/ 22 /2020 1.0 Outlined functionality Kelly Faria 06/01/2020 2.0 Include On/Off functionality for previous 12 weeks Kelly Faria 07/01/2020 3.0 U Made u pdates based on R&D recommendation s to sections 3.2.1, 3.3.1, 3.3.2.1, 3.3.3.2 Kelly Faria 07/11/2020 4.0 Updates based on R&D recommendations Added Enablement sections Kelly Faria 07 /15/2020 5.0 Update to Recent Hours calculation by ASYNC batch and example scenario EX23 – Training Hours Tracking This extension includes enhancements to the user Interface, the database, and the logic behind the ReprocessCICO Processor, ASYNCWMOSEventProcessingBatch, and Perf EvaluationProcess Batch to calculates and appl y a n employee’s performance goal based on how many hours they’ve worked. This extension enables the L abor M anagement application to track Training Hours for a J ob Function Level grouping instead of a single job function giving McLane the ability t o keep their current JF structure for gra nular Labor Management reporting , whi le also tracking training hours at a higher level of similar Job Functions. This extension also allows the system to look back only at the previous X weeks (based on purge config) for training hours instead of all time. Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) Y Changes to Job Function UI and Employee Training Requirements UI MHE N RF N Reporting N Other Y DB Changes to JF Training Tables Changes to ReprocessCICO , ASYNC, and PerfEvaluationProcess batches Changes to Purge Codes This extension will allow McLane to move the tracking of training hours and the ability to move employees along a training curve from Job Function to a JF Level Grouping. Assumptions Base level configuration will be utilized to configure a hierarchy above the Job Function . Level 1 will hold the Job Function Training levels. Job Functions are assigned the appropriate Level 1 grouping via the Job Functions UI. A Job Function cannot be assigned to multiple training curve levels ( ie . Multiple Level 1 groupings) Training Hours will only be tracked for Job Functions with Level 1 configured. If the extension is enabled and the ‘Is Level Training Req uired?’ flag is checked, the Job Function must have a Level 1 value assigned. To avoid missing configuration , ensure to assign a Level 1 value to a Job Function before updating the training requirements tab. If training curves are used at a Job Function level , the ability to track training at individual J ob F unctions disappears unless each Job Function is assigned to its own level . Levels must be configured via the System Code L-021 to support this functionality. EX23 logic applies only to events are processed via ASYNCWMOSEv entProcessingBatch (ex. RF events, event interface), not manually created events. Hardware is sized to accommodate 12 weeks of E_EMP_JF_WORK_DTL records (1 record per employee per clock in per JF). A ll Job Functions within the same level must have the same configuration for Consider OS Hours Only. IntradayProcessor batch is disabled . Team Standard or Reflective Standard Job Functions are not configured for Level Training Curves. Base 2-Way Calling does not take Training Curves into account. User is presented with SAM value (unadjusted time) as Estimated Time. Labor Planning, Forecasting, Scheduling, and Incentives are not in scope for this extension . Dependencies N/A P latform Information Solution: Solution Platform: Solution Version: Warehouse Management Open Systems Oracle/ Tomcat /RHEL 201 9 Functionality This extension enables the Labor Management application to track Training Hours for a Job Function Level grouping instead of a single job function. This extension includes enhancements to the user i nterface, database, purge codes, and certain LM batches to calculate and apply an employee’s performance goal based on how many hours they’ve worked in a specific Level . This extension also allows the system to look back only at the previous X weeks for training hours instead of all time. UI Changes As part of the EX23 functionality, 3 base UI screens are customized to support configuration and tracking of Level Training curves. Enablement: IF C-000-000 byte 1 = Y (global extension enablement)  IF C-000-EX23 byte 1 = Y (extension specific enablement)  THEN all changes in this section (3.1) are enabled ELSE the changes in this section (3.1) are disabled and base functionality/display is used ELSE the functionality detailed in this section is disabled  ELSE the functionality detailed in this section is disabled  Job Function UI Display Changes The Job Function Training Requirements Tab in the Job Functions UI is utilized to configure parameters such as Training Period and Retraining Period . Users will configure this screen to align with the parameters for the Job Function’s corresponding Level 1 Training Curve. The following labels on the Job Function Training Requirements tab are customized to refer to ‘Level’: Base UI Field Custom UI Field Job Function Training Requirements (Tab) Level Training Requirements Is Job Function Training Required? Is Level Training Required Elapsed Duration Without Working in Job Function to Require Retraining (in Days) Elapsed Duration Without Working in Level to Require Retraining (in Days) Additional Logic Additionally, logic is added when a user saves changes on the Job Function UI screen. If a user saves changes to an existing Job Function , custom logic is triggered to evaluate which Level 1 the Job Function belongs to (E_JOB_FUNCTION.LEVEL_1) and update any other Job Functions assigned to that level with the same parameters . If a new Job Function is being created , LM will not update any other Job Functions, but instead populate the fields on the newly created Job Function based on an existing Job Function assigned to the same Level 1 grouping (E_JOB_FUNCTION.LEVEL_1). If Level 1 is null in either scenario above, no custom DB updates are made. In either the scenario s above, LM populates or updates the following fields in the E_JOB_FUNCTION: E_JOB_FUNCTION Field UI Field TRAIN_PERIOD Training Period (in hours) RETRAIN_PERIOD Retraining Period (in hours) OS_ONLY Consider on Standard Hours Only RETRAIN_REQ_DURATION Elapsed Duration Without Working in Job Function to Require Retraining (in Days) PERF_GOAL_IND 100% Performance Goal Required when Employee Achieves Standard TRAINING_REQD Is Job Function Training Required? USE_JF_TRAIN PERF_EVAL_PERIOD_ID Performance Goal Evaluation Period Example Scenario – Update an Existing Job Function Job Function Name Level 1 PUTAWAY COOLER Putaway PUTAWAY FREEZER Putaway User configures the Job Function Training Requirements Tab for PUTAWAY COOLER Job Function: Upon saving, custom logic Determines PUTAWAY COOLER is assigned to Level 1 Putaway Determines that PUTAWAY FREEZER is also assigned to Level 1 Putaway Updates the E_JOB_FUNCTION fields listed above for PUTAWAY FREEZER to match PUTAWAY COOLER Example Scenario – New Job Function Job Function Name Level 1 PUTAWAY COOLER Putaway PUTAWAY FREEZER Putaway User creates a new Job Function of PUTAWAY DRY: Upon saving, custom logic Determines PUTAWAY DRY is assigned to Level 1 Putaway Determines that PUTAWAY COOLER is also assigned to Level 1 Putaway Find the Job Function with the lowest JOB_FUNC_ID in E_JOB_FUNCTION where LEVEL_1 = PUTAWAY Updates the E_JOB_FUNCTION fields listed above for PUTAWAY DRY to match PUTAWAY COOLER Job Function Performance Goals UI Display Changes The Job Function Performance Goals page , reached via the Job Functions UI > More > Performance Goal , is utilized to configure the steps of the Training and Retraining Curves and corresponding Performance Goals. Users will configure this screen to align with the parameters for the corresponding Level 1 Training Curve. The following labels on the Job Function Performance Goals page are customized to refer to ‘Level’: Base UI Field Custom UI Field Job Function Performance Goals (UI name) Level Performance Goals Additional Logic Additionally, logic is added when a user saves changes on this screen. If a user saves changes to an existing Job Function, custom logic is triggered to evaluate which Level 1 the Job Function belongs to (E_JOB_FUNCTION.LEVEL_1) and create or update E_JOB_FUNC_PERF_GOAL records for any other Job Functions assigned to that level to match the configured Training Curve. If Level 1 is null, no custom DB updates are made. Example Scenario Job Function Name Level 1 PUTAWAY COOLER Putaway PUTAWAY FREEZER Putaway User configures the Job Function Training Curve for PUTAWAY COOLER Job Function: Upon saving, custom logic Determines PUTAWAY COOLER is assigned to Level 1 Putaway Determines that PUTAWAY FREEZER is also assigned to Level 1 Putaway Find the Job Function with the lowest JOB_FUNC_ID in E_JOB_FUNCTION where LEVEL_1 = PUTAWAY Inserts or updates E_JOB_FUNC_PERF_GOAL records for PUTAWAY FREEZER to match PUTAWAY COOLER Employee Training Requirements The Employee Training Requirements UI is updated to track employees who have been fully trained by Level 1 instead of by Job Function. Display Changes The UI column for ‘Job Function’ is removed. Instead, a new UI column is created for ‘Level 1’ which displays the values held in E_EMP_TRAIN_OVERRIDE.MISC_TXT_1. The ‘Job Function’ UI filter is updated to a ‘Level 1’ filter. Adding a Record When adding a record to this screen, the required ‘Job Function’ prompt is updated to a ‘Level 1’ prompt which pulls from the L-021 System Code IDs to populate the drop down list. The Job Function DB field, E_EMP_TRAIN_OVERRIDE.JOB_FUNC_ID, is defaulted to MANH Job Function upon insertion. DB Table Changes E_EMP_JF_WORK The E_EMP_JF_WORK table is where LM stores the previous hours worked in each JF by employee . EX23 customizes this database table to include an additional column for LEVEL_1. This allows LM to sum all of the records with the same LEVEL_1 value to determine where on the Training Curve employees should be. DB Table Added Column Data Type Populated By E_EMP_JF_WORK LEVEL_1 VARCHAR2(15) E_JOB_FUNCTION.LEVEL_1 EX23 also adds an additional column for RECENT_TRAIN_HRS. This column will be populated with the sum of related records in E_EMP_JF_WORK_DTL to allow PREV_TRAIN_HRS to not be impacted by custom functionality. DB Table Added Column Data Type Populated By E_EMP_JF_WORK RECENT_TRAIN_HRS NUMBER(20,7) Custom logic in 3.3.2.1 Process Flow In order to hold employees to a Training Curve at the Level 1 level, LM tracks how many hours an employee has worked in that Level 1 grouping previously and uses that value to determine what their Performance Goal should be based on the Training Curve configuration. In order to accomplish this, the ReprocessCICO Processor , ASYNC WMOSEventProcessingBatch , and PerfEvaluationProcess Batch are customized to track and apply the configured Training Curves at a Level 1 level. Storing Previous Training Hours for X weeks Th e E_EMP_JF_WORK_DTL table is included in the base Employee Clock In Clock Out Purge Code . Initially, the Employee Clock In Clock Out Purge Code is configured to maintain data, including E_EMP_JF_WORK_DTL data , for 12 weeks. In future, if changes are made to this time frame, confirm impact with Hardware Sizing team. ReprocessCICO Processor Updates The ReprocessCICOProcessor batch runs at a Frequency to reprocess employee s’ Clock In records and generate / update E_EMP_JF_WORK_DTL and E_EMP_JF_WORK records based on work hours accumulated through the day. Enablement: IF C-000-000 byte 1 = Y (global extension enablement)  IF C-000-EX23 byte 1 = Y (extension specific enablement)  THEN functionality detailed in this section (3.3.2) is enabled ELSE functionality detailed in this ty in this section is disabled ELSE the functionality detailed in this section is disabled  ELSE the functionality detailed in this section is disabled  All Time Training Hours vs. Limited Training Hours Populate RECENT_TRAIN_HRS McLane has a requirement to only include the previous 12 weeks of data when calculating which level of the training curve an employee should be. To meet that requirement, the following changes are made to the Reprocess CICO batch: When Reprocess CICO batch runs, LM is customized to populate/replace E_EMP_JF_WORK. RECENT_TRAIN_HRS with the sum of Clock In hours in E_EMP_JF_WORK_DTL for that employee and Job Function . This allows the system to track hours based on data in E_EMP_JF_WORK_DTL (RECENT_TRAIN_HRS) as well as all time (PREV_TRAIN_HRS) . PREV_TRAIN_HRS and PREV_RETRAIN_HRS columns are is not affected. Populate LEVEL_1 ReprocessCICO is also modified to populate an additional column in E_EMP_JF_WORK to allow tracking Previous Trained Hours by Level 1. This custom DB column is detailed in section 3.2.1 – E_EMP_JF_WORK.LEVEL_1 . This column should be validated/populated every time the ReprocessCICO batch runs to account for configuration changes to E_JOB_FUNCTION.LEVEL_1. ASYNC WMOSEventProcessing Batch Updates The ASYNCWMOSEventProcessingBatch runs at a Frequency to process new Labor events through base LM calculations, including applying Training Curve Performance Goals. When the ASYNC Batch runs for an event, the following custom logic is triggered: Evaluate E_EMP_TRAIN_OVERRIDE First, ASYNC batch references Employee Training Requirements (E_EMP_TRAIN_OVERRIDE) to determine if the user has been marked as fully trained for the event’s corresponding Job Function Level (E_EMP_TR AI N_OVERRIDE.MISC_TXT_1). One record is stored here per Employee/Level 1 combination. ASYNC batch references MISC_TXT_1 based on mod enablement detailed below , regardles s of the custom enablement for section 3.3. 3 . 2 . If the Even t’s corresponding Job Function does not have a value populated in E_JOB_FUNCTION.LEVEL_1, do not continue to the logic in section 3.3.3.2 and do not apply a performance goal to the event. Enablement: IF C-000-000 byte 1 = Y (global extension enablement)  IF C-000-EX23 byte 1 = Y (extension specific enablement)  THEN functionality detailed in this section (3.3.3.1) is enabled ELSE functionality detailed in this ty in this section is disabled ELSE the functionality detailed in this section is disabled  ELSE the functionality detailed in this section is disabled  E_EMP_JF_WORK Apply Performance Goal If the employee has not been marked as fully trained for this Level, custom logic sums all E_EMP_JF_WORK records for that employee with the same LEVEL_1 column as the event’s Job Function to calculate Recent Train Hours and Clock in Hours . Once the Recent Train Hours + Clock In Hours value is determined for the Level , LM base logic references E_JOB_FUNC_PERF_GOAL for the event’s corresponding Job Function to determine how far along the training curve the employee is. This determines what Performance Goal to apply to the event. Changes in 3.3.3.2 are controlled by a custom system code C-X23-Limit Training Hrs so that MCLN may choose to return to All Time Training Hours tracking as per base while keeping the rest of EX23 logic enabled. Enablement: IF C-000-000 byte 1 = Y (global extension enablement)  IF C-000-EX23 byte 1 = Y (extension specific enablement)  IF C-X23-Limit Train Hrs byte 1= Y (functionality specific enablement) THEN ASYNC batch references RECENT_TRAIN_HRS + CLOCK_IN_HRS   ELSE  ASYNC batch uses base logic t o reference PREV_TRAIN_HRS + CLOCK_IN_HRS ELSE the functionality detailed in this section is disabled ELSE the functionality detailed in this section is disabled  ELSE the functionality detailed in this section is disabled  PerfEvaluationProcessBatch Updates The PerfEvaluationProcessBatch runs at a Frequency to determine if an employee has performed at 100% or higher during their training period for the designated duration. This is controlled by Job Function configuration: 100% Perf Goal Required when Employee Achieves Standard and Performance Goal Evaluation Period . If the employee has performed at 100% or higher during that time, the batch creates a record in E_EMP_TRAIN_OVERRIDE (Employee Training Requirements UI) indicating that the employee is fully trained. When the PerfEvaluation batch runs, custom logic evaluates E_CONSOL records for all Job Functions with the same LEVEL_1 group value to determine if the employee has achieved 100% or more Employee Performance. When inserting a record into E_EMP_TRAIN_OVERRIDE, PerfEvaluation Batch hard codes the JOB_FUNC_ID to MANH Job Function and populates LEVEL_1 in MISC_TXT_1. Only 1 record is generated per user and Level 1 combination into this table. Enablement: IF C-000-000 byte 1 = Y (global extension enablement)  IF C-000-EX23 byte 1 = Y (extension specific enablement)  THEN functionality detailed in this section (3.3.1) is enabled ELSE functionality detailed in this section is disabled ELSE the functionality detailed in this section is disabled  ELSE the functionality detailed in this section is disabled  Example Employee on Training Curve w orks in m ultiple Job Functions assigned to the same Level 1 The following Job Functions are configured : Job Function Name Level 1 PUTAWAY COOLER Putaway PUTAWAY FREEZER Putaway The Job Functions are configured to require JF Training and the Training Curve is configured for Level 1 Putaway: Custom System Code C-X23-Limit Train Hrs parameter is set to ‘Y’ to enable custom ASYNC batch logic to reference RECENT_TRAIN_HRS instead of PREV_TRAIN_HRS. JSMITH has the following records in E_EMP_JF_WORK_DTL and E_EMP_JF_WORK which show how many hours he’s worked in these Job Functions previously : E_EMP_JF_WORK_DTL – unique record by User , JF, Clock In record E_EMP_JF_WORK_ID Clock In Day PREV_TRAIN_HRS CLOCK_IN_HRS 1 5/20/2020 0 3.75 1 5/21/2020 3.75 2.5 2 5/21/2020 0 3.0 E_EMP_JF_WORK – unique record by User , JF E_EMP_JF_WORK_ID User Job Function Level 1 PREV_TRAIN_HRS CLOCK_IN_HRS RECENT_TRAIN_HRS 1 JSMITH PUTAWAY FREEZER Putaway 3.75 2.5 6.25 3.75 2 JSMITH PUTAWAY COOLER Putaway 0 3.0 0 3.0 JSMITH clocks in the next day and performs a PUTAWAY FREEZER event . When the ASYNC batch runs, the following logic happens: ASYNC references Employee Training Requirements (E_EMP_TRAIN_OVERRIDE) and sees that JSMITH is not marked as trained for Putaway Level 1 ASYNC evaluates that Custom System Code C-X23-Limit Train Hrs parameter is set to ‘Y’ which determines that ASYNC will reference RECENT_TRAIN_HRS instead of PREV_TRAIN_HRS in the next step ASYNC sums all E_EMP_JF_WORK records for Level 1 = Putaway SUM( RECENT_ PREV_ TRAIN_HRS) + SUM(CLOCK_IN_HRS) = 3.75 + 2.5 6.25 + 3 = 9.25 ASYNC compares the 9.25 hours against the configured training curve and determines that Employee is on the first tier. A Performance Goal of 80% is applied to JSMITH’s event With the Performance Goal applied to JSMITH’s event, his PAM is calculated as 7.5 min (6 min SAM / .80 performance goal) Tran # User Activity Job Function SAM (Standard Allowable Minutes, unadjusted) PAM (Performance Allowable Minutes, adjusted) 1000 JSMITH PUTAWAY FREEZER PUTAWAY FREEZER 6 7.5 JSMITH’s PAM is used for base labor reporting to calculate and show his Adjusted Performance. Other Solutions Included in Extension Not in scope for this extension. Required Data Setup All necessary data setup for the extension to function accurately are detailed below. General Additional Facility Configuration In Additional Facility Configuration UI , in the Calculations tab, configure the Em ployee Performance Goal Source parameter as ‘ Hours Worked ’ . Batch Configuration In Batch Process Inquiry UI , c onfigure the following batches according to Manhattan R&D recommendations and ensure they are in ‘Waiting to Run’ status : ASYNCWMOSEventProcessingBatch PerfEvaluationProcessBatch ReprocessCICOProcessor Job Function Level Configuration In System Codes UI , c onfigure Level 1 groups in System Code L-021 per the Design Matrix and section 5.4.2 Job Function Configuration In Job Functions UI , c onfigure all Job Functions where Training Curves will be utilized and select a Level 1 value . Job Function Performance Goal Configuration In Job Functions UI > More > Performance Goal, configure the Training Curves for the Level 1 grouping corresponding to the Job Function. Menu Parameters N/A Warehouse Master N/A System Codes Extension Enablement System Codes Code ID Details Parameter Configuration Rec Type Code Type Code ID Short Description Description Frm To Description Validation Type Configured value C 000 000 Global Extension Control Global Extension Control 1 1 Enablement Flag Check Box Y/N Flag Y – Global Extension Control is turned ON C 000 EX 23 EX 23 On/Off EX 23 On/Off 1 1 Enablement Flag Check Box Y/N Flag Y – EX 23 Extension Control is turned ON C X23 Limit Train Hrs Limit Hrs Limit Train Hrs 1 1 Limit Training Hrs Check Box Y/N Flag Y – Section 3.3.2.1 is enabled Level 1 Groups System Co d e L-021 stores the Level 1 values to populate the drop down in Job Functions UI . Kelly Faria 05/ 22 /2020 1.0 Outlined functionality Kelly Faria 06/01/2020 2.0 Include On/Off functionality for previous 12 weeks Kelly Faria 07/01/2020 3.0 U Made u pdates based on R&D recommendation s to sections 3.2.1, 3.3.1, 3.3.2.1, 3.3.3.2 Kelly Faria 07/11/2020 4.0 Updates based on R&D recommendations Added Enablement sections Kelly Faria 07 /15/2020 5.0 Update to Recent Hours calculation by ASYNC batch and example scenario EX23 – Training Hours Tracking This extension includes enhancements to the user Interface, the database, and the logic behind the ReprocessCICO Processor, ASYNCWMOSEventProcessingBatch, and Perf EvaluationProcess Batch to calculates and appl y a n employee’s performance goal based on how many hours they’ve worked. This extension enables the L abor M anagement application to track Training Hours for a J ob Function Level grouping instead of a single job function giving McLane the ability t o keep their current JF structure for gra nular Labor Management reporting , whi le also tracking training hours at a higher level of similar Job Functions. This extension also allows the system to look back only at the previous X weeks (based on purge config) for training hours instead of all time. Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) Y Changes to Job Function UI and Employee Training Requirements UI MHE N RF N Reporting N Other Y DB Changes to JF Training Tables Changes to ReprocessCICO , ASYNC, and PerfEvaluationProcess batches Changes to Purge Codes This extension will allow McLane to move the tracking of training hours and the ability to move employees along a training curve from Job Function to a JF Level Grouping. Assumptions Base level configuration will be utilized to configure a hierarchy above the Job Function . Level 1 will hold the Job Function Training levels. Job Functions are assigned the appropriate Level 1 grouping via the Job Functions UI. A Job Function cannot be assigned to multiple training curve levels ( ie . Multiple Level 1 groupings) Training Hours will only be tracked for Job Functions with Level 1 configured. If the extension is enabled and the ‘Is Level Training Req uired?’ flag is checked, the Job Function must have a Level 1 value assigned. To avoid missing configuration , ensure to assign a Level 1 value to a Job Function before updating the training requirements tab.

[Document: MCLN EX23 - Training Hours Tracking]

tions with Level 1 configured. If the extension is enabled and the ‘Is Level Training Req uired?’ flag is checked, the Job Function must have a Level 1 value assigned. To avoid missing configuration , ensure to assign a Level 1 value to a Job Function before updating the training requirements tab.

If training curves are used at a Job Function level , the ability to track training at individual J ob F unctions disappears unless each Job Function is assigned to its own level . Levels must be configured via the System Code L-021 to support this functionality. EX23 logic applies only to events are processed via ASYNCWMOSEv entProcessingBatch (ex. RF events, event interface), not manually created events. Hardware is sized to accommodate 12 weeks of E_EMP_JF_WORK_DTL records (1 record per employee per clock in per JF). A ll Job Functions within the same level must have the same configuration for Consider OS Hours Only. IntradayProcessor batch is disabled . Team Standard or Reflective Standard Job Functions are not configured for Level Training Curves. Base 2-Way Calling does not take Training Curves into account. User is presented with SAM value (unadjusted time) as Estimated Time. Labor Planning, Forecasting, Scheduling, and Incentives are not in scope for this extension . Dependencies N/A P latform Information Solution: Solution Platform: Solution Version: Warehouse Management Open Systems Oracle/ Tomcat /RHEL 201 9 Functionality This extension enables the Labor Management application to track Training Hours for a Job Function Level grouping instead of a single job function. This extension includes enhancements to the user i nterface, database, purge codes, and certain LM batches to calculate and apply an employee’s performance goal based on how many hours they’ve worked in a specific Level . This extension also allows the system to look back only at the previous X weeks for training hours instead of all time. UI Changes As part of the EX23 functionality, 3 base UI screens are customized to support configuration and tracking of Level Training curves. Enablement: IF C-000-000 byte 1 = Y (global extension enablement)  IF C-000-EX23 byte 1 = Y (extension specific enablement)  THEN all changes in this section (3.1) are enabled ELSE the changes in this section (3.1) are disabled and base functionality/display is used ELSE the functionality detailed in this section is disabled  ELSE the functionality detailed in this section is disabled  Job Function UI Display Changes The Job Function Training Requirements Tab in the Job Functions UI is utilized to configure parameters such as Training Period and Retraining Period . Users will configure this screen to align with the parameters for the Job Function’s corresponding Level 1 Training Curve.

[Document: MCLN EX23 - Training Hours Tracking]

ction UI Display Changes The Job Function Training Requirements Tab in the Job Functions UI is utilized to configure parameters such as Training Period and Retraining Period . Users will configure this screen to align with the parameters for the Job Function’s corresponding Level 1 Training Curve.

The following labels on the Job Function Training Requirements tab are customized to refer to ‘Level’: Base UI Field Custom UI Field Job Function Training Requirements (Tab) Level Training Requirements Is Job Function Training Required? Is Level Training Required Elapsed Duration Without Working in Job Function to Require Retraining (in Days) Elapsed Duration Without Working in Level to Require Retraining (in Days) Additional Logic Additionally, logic is added when a user saves changes on the Job Function UI screen. If a user saves changes to an existing Job Function , custom logic is triggered to evaluate which Level 1 the Job Function belongs to (E_JOB_FUNCTION.LEVEL_1) and update any other Job Functions assigned to that level with the same parameters . If a new Job Function is being created , LM will not update any other Job Functions, but instead populate the fields on the newly created Job Function based on an existing Job Function assigned to the same Level 1 grouping (E_JOB_FUNCTION.LEVEL_1). If Level 1 is null in either scenario above, no custom DB updates are made. In either the scenario s above, LM populates or updates the following fields in the E_JOB_FUNCTION: E_JOB_FUNCTION Field UI Field TRAIN_PERIOD Training Period (in hours) RETRAIN_PERIOD Retraining Period (in hours) OS_ONLY Consider on Standard Hours Only RETRAIN_REQ_DURATION Elapsed Duration Without Working in Job Function to Require Retraining (in Days) PERF_GOAL_IND 100% Performance Goal Required when Employee Achieves Standard TRAINING_REQD Is Job Function Training Required?

D Retraining Period (in hours) OS_ONLY Consider on Standard Hours Only RETRAIN_REQ_DURATION Elapsed Duration Without Working in Job Function to Require Retraining (in Days) PERF_GOAL_IND 100% Performance Goal Required when Employee Achieves Standard TRAINING_REQD Is Job Function Training Required?

USE_JF_TRAIN PERF_EVAL_PERIOD_ID Performance Goal Evaluation Period Example Scenario – Update an Existing Job Function Job Function Name Level 1 PUTAWAY COOLER Putaway PUTAWAY FREEZER Putaway User configures the Job Function Training Requirements Tab for PUTAWAY COOLER Job Function: Upon saving, custom logic Determines PUTAWAY COOLER is assigned to Level 1 Putaway Determines that PUTAWAY FREEZER is also assigned to Level 1 Putaway Updates the E_JOB_FUNCTION fields listed above for PUTAWAY FREEZER to match PUTAWAY COOLER Example Scenario – New Job Function Job Function Name Level 1 PUTAWAY COOLER Putaway PUTAWAY FREEZER Putaway User creates a new Job Function of PUTAWAY DRY: Upon saving, custom logic Determines PUTAWAY DRY is assigned to Level 1 Putaway Determines that PUTAWAY COOLER is also assigned to Level 1 Putaway Find the Job Function with the lowest JOB_FUNC_ID in E_JOB_FUNCTION where LEVEL_1 = PUTAWAY Updates the E_JOB_FUNCTION fields listed above for PUTAWAY DRY to match PUTAWAY COOLER Job Function Performance Goals UI Display Changes The Job Function Performance Goals page , reached via the Job Functions UI > More > Performance Goal , is utilized to configure the steps of the Training and Retraining Curves and corresponding Performance Goals. Users will configure this screen to align with the parameters for the corresponding Level 1 Training Curve. The following labels on the Job Function Performance Goals page are customized to refer to ‘Level’: Base UI Field Custom UI Field Job Function Performance Goals (UI name) Level Performance Goals Additional Logic Additionally, logic is added when a user saves changes on this screen. If a user saves changes to an existing Job Function, custom logic is triggered to evaluate which Level 1 the Job Function belongs to (E_JOB_FUNCTION.LEVEL_1) and create or update E_JOB_FUNC_PERF_GOAL records for any other Job Functions assigned to that level to match the configured Training Curve. If Level 1 is null, no custom DB updates are made.

[Document: MCLN EX23 - Training Hours Tracking]

, custom logic is triggered to evaluate which Level 1 the Job Function belongs to (E_JOB_FUNCTION.LEVEL_1) and create or update E_JOB_FUNC_PERF_GOAL records for any other Job Functions assigned to that level to match the configured Training Curve. If Level 1 is null, no custom DB updates are made.

Example Scenario Job Function Name Level 1 PUTAWAY COOLER Putaway PUTAWAY FREEZER Putaway User configures the Job Function Training Curve for PUTAWAY COOLER Job Function: Upon saving, custom logic Determines PUTAWAY COOLER is assigned to Level 1 Putaway Determines that PUTAWAY FREEZER is also assigned to Level 1 Putaway Find the Job Function with the lowest JOB_FUNC_ID in E_JOB_FUNCTION where LEVEL_1 = PUTAWAY Inserts or updates E_JOB_FUNC_PERF_GOAL records for PUTAWAY FREEZER to match PUTAWAY COOLER Employee Training Requirements The Employee Training Requirements UI is updated to track employees who have been fully trained by Level 1 instead of by Job Function. Display Changes The UI column for ‘Job Function’ is removed. Instead, a new UI column is created for ‘Level 1’ which displays the values held in E_EMP_TRAIN_OVERRIDE.MISC_TXT_1. The ‘Job Function’ UI filter is updated to a ‘Level 1’ filter. Adding a Record When adding a record to this screen, the required ‘Job Function’ prompt is updated to a ‘Level 1’ prompt which pulls from the L-021 System Code IDs to populate the drop down list. The Job Function DB field, E_EMP_TRAIN_OVERRIDE.JOB_FUNC_ID, is defaulted to MANH Job Function upon insertion. DB Table Changes E_EMP_JF_WORK The E_EMP_JF_WORK table is where LM stores the previous hours worked in each JF by employee . EX23 customizes this database table to include an additional column for LEVEL_1. This allows LM to sum all of the records with the same LEVEL_1 value to determine where on the Training Curve employees should be. DB Table Added Column Data Type Populated By E_EMP_JF_WORK LEVEL_1 VARCHAR2(15) E_JOB_FUNCTION.LEVEL_1 EX23 also adds an additional column for RECENT_TRAIN_HRS. This column will be populated with the sum of related records in E_EMP_JF_WORK_DTL to allow PREV_TRAIN_HRS to not be impacted by custom functionality. DB Table Added Column Data Type Populated By E_EMP_JF_WORK RECENT_TRAIN_HRS NUMBER(20,7) Custom logic in 3.3.2.1 Process Flow In order to hold employees to a Training Curve at the Level 1 level, LM tracks how many hours an employee has worked in that Level 1 grouping previously and uses that value to determine what their Performance Goal should be based on the Training Curve configuration. In order to accomplish this, the ReprocessCICO Processor , ASYNC WMOSEventProcessingBatch , and PerfEvaluationProcess Batch are customized to track and apply the configured Training Curves at a Level 1 level.

[Document: MCLN EX23 - Training Hours Tracking]

etermine what their Performance Goal should be based on the Training Curve configuration. In order to accomplish this, the ReprocessCICO Processor , ASYNC WMOSEventProcessingBatch , and PerfEvaluationProcess Batch are customized to track and apply the configured Training Curves at a Level 1 level.

Storing Previous Training Hours for X weeks Th e E_EMP_JF_WORK_DTL table is included in the base Employee Clock In Clock Out Purge Code . Initially, the Employee Clock In Clock Out Purge Code is configured to maintain data, including E_EMP_JF_WORK_DTL data , for 12 weeks. In future, if changes are made to this time frame, confirm impact with Hardware Sizing team. ReprocessCICO Processor Updates The ReprocessCICOProcessor batch runs at a Frequency to reprocess employee s’ Clock In records and generate / update E_EMP_JF_WORK_DTL and E_EMP_JF_WORK records based on work hours accumulated through the day. Enablement: IF C-000-000 byte 1 = Y (global extension enablement)  IF C-000-EX23 byte 1 = Y (extension specific enablement)  THEN functionality detailed in this section (3.3.2) is enabled ELSE functionality detailed in this ty in this section is disabled ELSE the functionality detailed in this section is disabled  ELSE the functionality detailed in this section is disabled  All Time Training Hours vs. Limited Training Hours Populate RECENT_TRAIN_HRS McLane has a requirement to only include the previous 12 weeks of data when calculating which level of the training curve an employee should be. To meet that requirement, the following changes are made to the Reprocess CICO batch: When Reprocess CICO batch runs, LM is customized to populate/replace E_EMP_JF_WORK. RECENT_TRAIN_HRS with the sum of Clock In hours in E_EMP_JF_WORK_DTL for that employee and Job Function . This allows the system to track hours based on data in E_EMP_JF_WORK_DTL (RECENT_TRAIN_HRS) as well as all time (PREV_TRAIN_HRS) . PREV_TRAIN_HRS and PREV_RETRAIN_HRS columns are is not affected. Populate LEVEL_1 ReprocessCICO is also modified to populate an additional column in E_EMP_JF_WORK to allow tracking Previous Trained Hours by Level 1. This custom DB column is detailed in section 3.2.1 – E_EMP_JF_WORK.LEVEL_1 . This column should be validated/populated every time the ReprocessCICO batch runs to account for configuration changes to E_JOB_FUNCTION.LEVEL_1. ASYNC WMOSEventProcessing Batch Updates The ASYNCWMOSEventProcessingBatch runs at a Frequency to process new Labor events through base LM calculations, including applying Training Curve Performance Goals.

[Document: MCLN EX23 - Training Hours Tracking]

the ReprocessCICO batch runs to account for configuration changes to E_JOB_FUNCTION.LEVEL_1. ASYNC WMOSEventProcessing Batch Updates The ASYNCWMOSEventProcessingBatch runs at a Frequency to process new Labor events through base LM calculations, including applying Training Curve Performance Goals.

When the ASYNC Batch runs for an event, the following custom logic is triggered: Evaluate E_EMP_TRAIN_OVERRIDE First, ASYNC batch references Employee Training Requirements (E_EMP_TRAIN_OVERRIDE) to determine if the user has been marked as fully trained for the event’s corresponding Job Function Level (E_EMP_TR AI N_OVERRIDE.MISC_TXT_1). One record is stored here per Employee/Level 1 combination. ASYNC batch references MISC_TXT_1 based on mod enablement detailed below , regardles s of the custom enablement for section 3.3. 3 . 2 . If the Even t’s corresponding Job Function does not have a value populated in E_JOB_FUNCTION.LEVEL_1, do not continue to the logic in section 3.3.3.2 and do not apply a performance goal to the event. Enablement: IF C-000-000 byte 1 = Y (global extension enablement)  IF C-000-EX23 byte 1 = Y (extension specific enablement)  THEN functionality detailed in this section (3.3.3.1) is enabled ELSE functionality detailed in this ty in this section is disabled ELSE the functionality detailed in this section is disabled  ELSE the functionality detailed in this section is disabled  E_EMP_JF_WORK Apply Performance Goal If the employee has not been marked as fully trained for this Level, custom logic sums all E_EMP_JF_WORK records for that employee with the same LEVEL_1 column as the event’s Job Function to calculate Recent Train Hours and Clock in Hours . Once the Recent Train Hours + Clock In Hours value is determined for the Level , LM base logic references E_JOB_FUNC_PERF_GOAL for the event’s corresponding Job Function to determine how far along the training curve the employee is. This determines what Performance Goal to apply to the event. Changes in 3.3.3.2 are controlled by a custom system code C-X23-Limit Training Hrs so that MCLN may choose to return to All Time Training Hours tracking as per base while keeping the rest of EX23 logic enabled.

[Document: MCLN EX23 - Training Hours Tracking]

ing curve the employee is. This determines what Performance Goal to apply to the event. Changes in 3.3.3.2 are controlled by a custom system code C-X23-Limit Training Hrs so that MCLN may choose to return to All Time Training Hours tracking as per base while keeping the rest of EX23 logic enabled.

Enablement: IF C-000-000 byte 1 = Y (global extension enablement)  IF C-000-EX23 byte 1 = Y (extension specific enablement)  IF C-X23-Limit Train Hrs byte 1= Y (functionality specific enablement) THEN ASYNC batch references RECENT_TRAIN_HRS + CLOCK_IN_HRS   ELSE  ASYNC batch uses base logic t o reference PREV_TRAIN_HRS + CLOCK_IN_HRS ELSE the functionality detailed in this section is disabled ELSE the functionality detailed in this section is disabled  ELSE the functionality detailed in this section is disabled  PerfEvaluationProcessBatch Updates The PerfEvaluationProcessBatch runs at a Frequency to determine if an employee has performed at 100% or higher during their training period for the designated duration. This is controlled by Job Function configuration: 100% Perf Goal Required when Employee Achieves Standard and Performance Goal Evaluation Period . If the employee has performed at 100% or higher during that time, the batch creates a record in E_EMP_TRAIN_OVERRIDE (Employee Training Requirements UI) indicating that the employee is fully trained. When the PerfEvaluation batch runs, custom logic evaluates E_CONSOL records for all Job Functions with the same LEVEL_1 group value to determine if the employee has achieved 100% or more Employee Performance. When inserting a record into E_EMP_TRAIN_OVERRIDE, PerfEvaluation Batch hard codes the JOB_FUNC_ID to MANH Job Function and populates LEVEL_1 in MISC_TXT_1. Only 1 record is generated per user and Level 1 combination into this table. Enablement: IF C-000-000 byte 1 = Y (global extension enablement)  IF C-000-EX23 byte 1 = Y (extension specific enablement)  THEN functionality detailed in this section (3.3.1) is enabled ELSE functionality detailed in this section is disabled ELSE the functionality detailed in this section is disabled  ELSE the functionality detailed in this section is disabled  Example Employee on Training Curve w orks in m ultiple Job Functions assigned to the same Level 1 The following Job Functions are configured : Job Function Name Level 1 PUTAWAY COOLER Putaway PUTAWAY FREEZER Putaway The Job Functions are configured to require JF Training and the Training Curve is configured for Level 1 Putaway: Custom System Code C-X23-Limit Train Hrs parameter is set to ‘Y’ to enable custom ASYNC batch logic to reference RECENT_TRAIN_HRS instead of PREV_TRAIN_HRS.

[Document: MCLN EX23 - Training Hours Tracking]

way PUTAWAY FREEZER Putaway The Job Functions are configured to require JF Training and the Training Curve is configured for Level 1 Putaway: Custom System Code C-X23-Limit Train Hrs parameter is set to ‘Y’ to enable custom ASYNC batch logic to reference RECENT_TRAIN_HRS instead of PREV_TRAIN_HRS.

JSMITH has the following records in E_EMP_JF_WORK_DTL and E_EMP_JF_WORK which show how many hours he’s worked in these Job Functions previously : E_EMP_JF_WORK_DTL – unique record by User , JF, Clock In record E_EMP_JF_WORK_ID Clock In Day PREV_TRAIN_HRS CLOCK_IN_HRS 1 5/20/2020 0 3.75 1 5/21/2020 3.75 2.5 2 5/21/2020 0 3.0 E_EMP_JF_WORK – unique record by User , JF E_EMP_JF_WORK_ID User Job Function Level 1 PREV_TRAIN_HRS CLOCK_IN_HRS RECENT_TRAIN_HRS 1 JSMITH PUTAWAY FREEZER Putaway 3.75 2.5 6.25 3.75 2 JSMITH PUTAWAY COOLER Putaway 0 3.0 0 3.0 JSMITH clocks in the next day and performs a PUTAWAY FREEZER event . When the ASYNC batch runs, the following logic happens: ASYNC references Employee Training Requirements (E_EMP_TRAIN_OVERRIDE) and sees that JSMITH is not marked as trained for Putaway Level 1 ASYNC evaluates that Custom System Code C-X23-Limit Train Hrs parameter is set to ‘Y’ which determines that ASYNC will reference RECENT_TRAIN_HRS instead of PREV_TRAIN_HRS in the next step ASYNC sums all E_EMP_JF_WORK records for Level 1 = Putaway SUM( RECENT_ PREV_ TRAIN_HRS) + SUM(CLOCK_IN_HRS) = 3.75 + 2.5 6.25 + 3 = 9.25 ASYNC compares the 9.25 hours against the configured training curve and determines that Employee is on the first tier. A Performance Goal of 80% is applied to JSMITH’s event With the Performance Goal applied to JSMITH’s event, his PAM is calculated as 7.5 min (6 min SAM / .80 performance goal) Tran # User Activity Job Function SAM (Standard Allowable Minutes, unadjusted) PAM (Performance Allowable Minutes, adjusted) 1000 JSMITH PUTAWAY FREEZER PUTAWAY FREEZER 6 7.5 JSMITH’s PAM is used for base labor reporting to calculate and show his Adjusted Performance. Other Solutions Included in Extension Not in scope for this extension. Required Data Setup All necessary data setup for the extension to function accurately are detailed below. General Additional Facility Configuration In Additional Facility Configuration UI , in the Calculations tab, configure the Em ployee Performance Goal Source parameter as ‘ Hours Worked ’ .

[Document: MCLN EX23 - Training Hours Tracking]

on. Required Data Setup All necessary data setup for the extension to function accurately are detailed below. General Additional Facility Configuration In Additional Facility Configuration UI , in the Calculations tab, configure the Em ployee Performance Goal Source parameter as ‘ Hours Worked ’ .

Batch Configuration In Batch Process Inquiry UI , c onfigure the following batches according to Manhattan R&D recommendations and ensure they are in ‘Waiting to Run’ status : ASYNCWMOSEventProcessingBatch PerfEvaluationProcessBatch ReprocessCICOProcessor Job Function Level Configuration In System Codes UI , c onfigure Level 1 groups in System Code L-021 per the Design Matrix and section 5.4.2 Job Function Configuration In Job Functions UI , c onfigure all Job Functions where Training Curves will be utilized and select a Level 1 value . Job Function Performance Goal Configuration In Job Functions UI > More > Performance Goal, configure the Training Curves for the Level 1 grouping corresponding to the Job Function.
